LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS PROVIDED PLAUSIBLE REASONS FOR THEIR TAXI RETIREMENT REGULATIONS: SAFETY, CLEANLINESS, AND COMFORT OF CABS, IMPROVEMENT OF PUBLIC SATISFACTION WITH TAXI SERVICES, LOWER EMISSIONS, AND PROMOTION OF THE TAXI INDUSTRY GENERALLY. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SHOW THAT ANY OF THE JUSTIFICATIONS IS IRRATIONAL. THE COURT ADDS, IN VIEW OF THE WARDEN CASE, DOES NOT RAISE TO LEAVE OF TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT FOR THE JURY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR #10276

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Larry R. Shockey, The Law Offices of Larry R. Shockey, (415) 584-0134, appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; Sherri Sokeland Kaiser, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4691, appearing for Defendant, CCSF, and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment is Granted. Defendants provided plausible reasons for their taxi retirement regulations: safety, cleanliness, and comfort of cabs, improvement of public satisfaction with taxi services, lower emissions, and promotion of the taxi industry generally. Plaintiff failed to show that any of the justifications is irrational. The Court adds, in view of the Warden case, does not raise to leave of triable issue of fact for the Jury. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/VC) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Jacqueline Chan, CSR 10276

Defendants provided plausible reasons for their taxi retirement regulations: safety, cleanliness, and comfort of cabs, improvement of public satisfaction with taxi services, lower emissions, and promotion of the taxi industry generally. Plaintiff failed to show that any of the justifications is irrational. The Court adds, in view of the Warden case, does not raise to leave of triable issue of fact for the Jury. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/VC) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Jacqueline Chan, CSR 10276
